items.title: Government considering further economic relief as motorists urged to reduce fuel use
items.summary: Transport Minister Catherine King has warned that even if the Strait of Hormuz opens soon, there's "a long tail" to the fuel crisis.

items.title: No deal: JD Vance leaves Pakistan after US and Iran fail to reach peace agreement
items.summary: Foreign Minister Penny Wong said it was "disappointing" that a deal had not been reached, urging the US and Iran to return to negotiations.

items.title: 'Beaten' and 'subject to mock executions': How protesters are being forgotten in war
items.summary: Several Iranians have been executed in recent weeks in Iran; some say the regime is sending the "most horrific warning signal".

items.title: Why Melbourne woman Ildi wants to see the end of Viktor Orbán's time ruling over Hungary
items.summary: From fencers to shopkeepers, Hungarians weigh in on an election that could end Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule.

items.title: 'Special thing to be a human': Artemis II crew welcomed home after moon mission
items.summary: The four astronauts took centre stage on Saturday, a day after returning from their historic lunar flyby.

items.title: 'A simpler way of being': The rise of the offline club
items.summary: There's a growing movement of young people coming together to switch off their phones and rediscover life offline.

items.title: Extreme conditions and a faulty space toilet. How Artemis II returned with Australia's help
items.summary: The mission faced engineering and human health challenges. Overcoming these obstacles in deep space was the whole point.

items.title: Why Pakistan is hosting the highest-level US-Iran talks in almost half a century
items.summary: Officials from the US and Iran are set to meet in Islamabad, with Pakistan hoping to broker an end to the war in the Middle East.

items.title: They were cleaning their homes in hope. By morning, the bombs had fallen
items.summary: Strikes in Lebanon continue despite a ceasefire, deepening uncertainty and the human cost of a war it did not choose.

items.title: Why experts seriously doubt the US' claim of 'overwhelming victory' in the war against Iran
items.summary: "Everyone loses in this war," one foreign policy analyst told SBS News.

items.title: Some Australians forced to choose 'between food and medicine', new survey finds
items.summary: A new survey has shed light on the financial decisions faced by some Australians prescribed medicines not covered by the PBS.

items.title: Iran and US still at odds on truce terms as negotiating teams arrive in Islamabad
items.summary: Iran says that to end the war in the Middle East, its assets must be unblocked and a ceasefire implemented in Lebanon.

items.title: How Australian backyard astronomers discovered a world-first planetary ring
items.summary: Amateur astronomers have been recognised for their discoveries that have "guided humanity’s big telescopes."

items.title: Australian War Memorial alters Ben Roberts-Smith display after war crime charges
items.summary: The Australian War Memorial said the display would remain on show but had been updated to reflect the allegations.

items.title: Colleagues pay tribute after Queensland MP Jimmy Sullivan, 44, found dead at home
items.summary: A state MP has been found dead at his home, prompting condolences to his family from all sides of politics.

items.summary: Hungary's election this weekend is "one of the most momentous elections in Europe and for Europe in many years," according to one analyst.

items.summary: Iran and the United States said the strait would reopen after the two-week truce was announced on Wednesday.

items.title: Israel agrees to Lebanon peace talks as strike deaths climb above 300
items.summary: The news comes a day after Israel launched its largest wave of strikes on Lebanon since 2 March.

items.title: Global foreign aid in freefall, and Australia is low on the list
items.summary: The OECD says that governments tightening their belts on foreign aid will mean hundreds of thousands of preventable deaths.

items.title: Russia and Ukraine agree to two-day Orthodox Easter ceasefire
items.summary: "People need an Easter without threats and a real move towards peace," Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said.

items.title: What Albanese's trip to Singapore could mean for Australia's fuel supply
items.summary: Albanese has warned that a lasting end to the war in the Middle East would not necessarily mean it's "back to business as usual".
